title: (No AI) What happens when AI starts building itself?
dc:creator: Russell Brandom
content: Richard Socher's new $650 million startup wants to build an AI that can research and improve itself indefinitely — and he insists it will actually ship products.

title: (No AI) Behold, the Elon Musk jackass trophy
author: Elizabeth Lopatto
content:
![Photo collage of Elon Musk and Sam Altman](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/STKE010_MuskvAltman_AParkin05.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Yesterday, in _Musk v. Altman_, before the jurors came in, Sam Altman's team passed up what looked - from a distance - like a little league trophy. It was not. Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers had the lawyers read the inscription aloud for the press: "Never stop being a jackass." It's a commemoration OpenAI employees bought for research scientist Josh Ackiam, who testified yesterday.

How exactly did this come up in [a trial about nonprofit contract law](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/917755/musk-altman-openai-xai-gossip)? Allegedly, when Elon Musk was leaving OpenAI, he talked about wanting to race ahead of Google. Achiam, who worked on AI safety, asked if that was really such a good idea. Musk called him a jackass. Years …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/930893/elon-musk-sam-altman-trial-ai-safety-jackass-statue)

Yesterday, in Musk v. Altman, before the jurors came in, Sam Altman's team passed up what looked - from a distance - like a little league trophy. It was not. Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers had the lawyers read the inscription aloud for the press: "Never stop being a jackass." It's a commemoration OpenAI employees bought for research scientist Josh Ackiam, who testified yesterday.
How exactly did this come up in a trial about nonprofit contract law? Allegedly, when Elon Musk was leaving OpenAI, he talked about wanting to race ahead of Google. Achiam, who worked on AI safety, asked if that was really such a good idea. Musk called him a jackass. Years …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) OpenAI’s Codex is now in the ChatGPT mobile app
author: Jay Peters
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/App-view.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

OpenAI is going to let users access Codex, its desktop AI tool that can write code and use apps on your computer, from the ChatGPT app on your phone.

Following the surge in popularity for Anthropic's Claude Code, OpenAI has been working quickly to try and catch up, including by cutting back [on "side quests,"](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/895835/openai-cuts-back-on-side-quests) shutting down projects like [the Sora video-generation tool](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/902368/openai-sora-dead-ai-video-generation-competition), and focusing on [growing its enterprise business](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/911118/openai-memo-cro-ai-competition-anthropic). The company's push included the recently released major update for Codex that lets it [operate apps on macOS](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/913034/openai-codex-updates-use-macos) - a potentially major step as part of its ambitions to [make a desktop "superapp."](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/897778/openai-chatgpt-codex-atlas-browser-superapp)

Codex in the ChatGPT mobile app lets …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/930763/openai-codex-chatgpt-ios-android-app-preview)

The company's push included the recently released major update for Codex that lets it operate apps on macOS - a potentially major step as part of its ambitions to make a desktop "superapp."

title: (No AI) Trump’s Tech Posse in China, Who’s Winning in Musk v. Altman, and Hantavirus Conspiracy Theories
